reviews.review_description:
This isn't a game as such, more of a fun little educational app that shows the relationship between mass and gravity.
It's a lot of fun to try to create the most extreme solar system possible. Chuck in 4 stars and a bunch of planets, and you'll witness pure chaos. 
You can also try out the creations of others in the leaderboard, and then add to their systems if you want.

Space Simulator 3D shines with its ability to amaze.
No need for intense action, just consistency, the silence of space, and the stars!
What I loved about Space Simulator 3D is that we finally land in infinity, without the pressure of gameplay.
We float among the stars, observe the planets, and glide between the nebulae. It's a cosmic meditation.
The graphics are immersive. The stellar textures, the lighting effects, and the view of planetary curves create a little sense of space.
The astrophysics, even if simplified, adds a welcome sense of credibility.
It's not just a starry background; the celestial bodies react (orbits, distances).
Nothing spectacular in terms of missions or objectives: it's the visual and contemplative journey that takes center stage.
I would have liked more close-up exploration of the planets.
But as a cosmic observation simulator, Space Simulator 3D fulfills its role of arousing awe very well.

​Lost in space, but in a good way! This VR game lets you build your own solar systems, and the 3D graphics are so good you'll feel like a cosmic architect. My biggest problem is that now I'm questioning why Pluto isn't a planet anymore. 10/10, would get lost in space again.

reviews.review_title: Fun and relaxing at the same time
reviews.review_description: I love it! Quite relaxing after a full day at work.

This is amazing! Creating planets with my own hands is almost too good to be true, I love it! Quite a lot of configuration options, stunning graphics, educational and fun to observe those gravitational patterns. Well done, really!
